title: Felicia brevifolia
taxonomy: Felicia brevifolia is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to South Africa. The plant is further classified within the genus Felicia, which belongs to the tribe Inuleae. The tribe Inuleae is a part of the subfamily Asteroideae. The plant's classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Tribe: Inuleae, Genus: Felicia, Species: F. brevifolia.

intro: Felicia brevifolia, also known as the Cape daisy or blue felicia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Felicia brevifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Felicia brevifolia are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
